qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded HTTP download allows denial of service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unbounded HTTP response.read() in the URL download path, not a pickle scanner detection bypass for getattr(builtins, "eval").

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Sensitive URL parameters logged in plaintext enabling credential theft
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns URL query parameter logging without redaction, not obfuscated eval detection in pickle analysis.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via unvalidated HTTP redirect chain allows access to internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unvalidated HTTP redirect following in _http_get(), not the pickle global/callable detection bypass described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ZipSlip path traversal in 7z archive extraction allows arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns 7z archive path traversal during extraction, not missing detection of a getattr-obfuscated builtins.eval callable in pickle payloads.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described picklescan root cause: malicious pickle payloads can hide a builtins.eval call behind getattr, causing picklescan to miss the dangerous callable. The final findings cover 7z extraction path traversal, HTTP redirect SSRF, unbounded HTTP download DoS, and URL query logging disclosure instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-9m3x-qqw2-h32h describes a picklescan detection bypass where getattr(builtins, "eval") hides an eval call nested under another callable in a pickle reduce path.
The final finding manifest lists four qualifying findings: dos_unbounded_http_download, info_disclosure_url_logging, ssrf_url_redirect, and zipslip_7z_archive_extraction.
None of the final qualifying reports names the advisory PoC pattern, builtins.eval, getattr-based callable obfuscation, pickle global detection, or the advisory patch/root-cause path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
